title: Buttermilk Biscuits
description: Papaw made these every Sunday morning. The secret is a hot oven and a gentle hand — the less you fuss with the dough, the taller they rise.
instructions: Whisk the flour, baking powder, and salt together in a large bowl.
Work the cold butter into the flour with your fingers until it looks like coarse crumbs.
Pour in the buttermilk and stir just until the dough comes together.
Turn the dough onto a floured counter, pat it about 3/4 inch thick, and cut out biscuits.
Bake on a baking sheet for 10 to 12 minutes, until golden on top.
notes: Handle the dough as little as possible — that keeps the biscuits tender.
No buttermilk? Stir 2 teaspoons of vinegar into regular milk and wait 5 minutes.
leftovers: Best the day they're made, but they keep 2 days in a bread box. Split and toast before serving.
storage: Cool completely, then store in a sealed bag on the counter. Freeze extras up to a month.
whyWeChoseThis: Only five ingredients, all pantry staples.
Papaw can make them from memory.
Cheaper than the canned kind and twice as good.

editorialNotes.note: First written edition of Papaw's Sunday biscuits.
